title: Understanding Precession
text: Precession is the slow movement of the axis of a spinning object around another axis due to an external force.

title: Formula for Precession Angular Velocity
text: The precession angular velocity \( \omega_p \) can be calculated using the formula: \( \omega_p $$=$$ \frac{mgr}{I\omega_s} \), where \( $$m$$ \) is the mass, \( g \) is the acceleration due to gravity, \( $$r$$ \) is the distance from the pivot, \( I \) is the moment of inertia, and \( \omega_s \) is the spin angular velocity.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the moment of inertia \( I \) of the disk using the formula \( I $$=$$ \frac{1}{2}mr**2 \).

title: Calculate Spin Angular Velocity
text: Convert the spin rate from revolutions per second to radians per second. Use the conversion \( $$1$$ \text{ rev} $$=$$ 2\pi \text{ rad} \).

title: Calculate Precession Angular Velocity
text: Substitute the values into the formula \( \omega_p $$=$$ \frac{mgr}{I\omega_s} \) to find the precession angular velocity.
